jokes.text: Son of a bitch! Oh, look what you did! You plopped it! I'll plop you!
jokes.setup: The Cornballer device burns users and creates father-son bonding moments
jokes.punchline: The absurd threat 'I'll plop you!' as retaliation for plopping cornballs
jokes.explanation: Strong wordplay with 'plop' as both verb for cornball-making and threat. Economy is excellent - maximum silly per word.

jokes.text: Never touch that.
jokes.setup: Michael warns about the dangerous Cornballer
jokes.punchline: The deadpan delivery of a safety warning about a breakfast appliance
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and character integration. Michael's overprotectiveness applied to a ridiculous kitchen device. Sets up running gag.

jokes.text: Or, you could not eat the cornballs and let your kid lead his own life.
jokes.setup: Michael talking about working off cornball calories
jokes.punchline: Lindsay connects dietary choices to helicopter parenting
jokes.explanation: Sharp structure connecting literal and metaphorical consumption. Perfect for Lindsay's character to make this observation.

jokes.text: Let's not blame the bike, okay? It's the poor carpenter that blames his shoddy tools for the... Ow!
jokes.setup: Michael delivers a wise saying about not blaming tools
jokes.punchline: Gets burned by the Cornballer mid-philosophy
jokes.explanation: Classic physical comedy interrupting pretentious wisdom. The Cornballer literally undermines Michael's point about tools.

jokes.text: That stupid cornballing piece of...
jokes.setup: Michael is frustrated with the Cornballer burning him
jokes.punchline: Uses 'cornballing' as a pseudo-curse word
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay turning the product name into a family-friendly curse. Shows escalating frustration with the device.

jokes.text: With what? Company credit card. You're returning that. Get a job.
jokes.setup: Lindsay claims she paid for shopping herself
jokes.punchline: Reveals she used the company credit card, then gets told to get a job
jokes.explanation: Solid character comedy showing Lindsay's financial delusion and Michael's exasperation. Good economy in the rapid-fire exchange.

jokes.text: I thought these things were only legal in Mexico. In fact, the Cornballer wasn't legal anywhere.
jokes.setup: Gob assumes the Cornballer is legal somewhere
jokes.punchline: Narrator reveals it's not legal anywhere
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation from Gob's assumption to the narrator's darker truth. Classic Arrested Development structure of undercutting character statements.

jokes.text: No borders. No limits. 'Go ahead, touch the Cornballer. You know best.'
jokes.setup: Michael sarcastically describes Tobias's parenting style
jokes.punchline: Uses the dangerous Cornballer as an example of letting kids make bad choices
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that transforms the Cornballer warning into a parenting philosophy critique. Shows Michael's character perfectly.

jokes.text: You're still not an actor.
jokes.setup: Tobias mentions his past as a psychiatrist before becoming an actor
jokes.punchline: Michael's blunt correction that he's still not an actor
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and timing. Michael's deadpan delivery cuts right through Tobias's self-delusion.

jokes.text: Am I touching something? Hot! Hot, hot. Hot.
jokes.setup: Tobias gives parenting advice about Michael being overbearing
jokes.punchline: Immediately burns himself on the very device Michael warned about
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic timing - Tobias critiques overprotective parenting while proving Michael's warnings were justified. Excellent structure.

jokes.text: Now watch this. Maeby, where are you off to on this glorious Sunday afternoon? She won't tell me. I'm going to audition for a play.
jokes.setup: Tobias demonstrates how his daughter won't share with him
jokes.punchline: She immediately shares when he asks
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that immediately undercuts Tobias's point. Shows his lack of self-awareness about his own parenting.

jokes.text: I was totally wrong. She's reaching out to her actor daddy.
jokes.setup: Maeby's going to audition for a play
jokes.punchline: Tobias thinks this validates his acting career and parenting theory
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Tobias's narcissism and delusion. He makes everything about himself.

jokes.text: And then we actually get to kiss, right?
jokes.setup: Steve Holt auditions for the romantic lead
jokes.punchline: His only concern is about the kissing scene
jokes.explanation: Solid character comedy showing Steve Holt's priorities and shallowness. Sets up the romantic triangle.

jokes.text: And then there's a kiss, right? Louder. And then there's a kiss, right?
jokes.setup: George Michael also auditions with similar motives
jokes.punchline: He repeats the same question louder, showing his awkwardness
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing George Michael's social awkwardness. The repetition and volume increase add to the cringe.

jokes.text: Steve Holt! Steve Holt! Stand-in for Steve Holt? Steve Holt!
jokes.setup: Cast list is posted
jokes.punchline: Steve Holt celebrates every mention of his name, even as a stand-in
jokes.explanation: Establishes Steve Holt's signature catchphrase/running gag. The escalating self-celebration is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: It'll fly out on its own. Why are you calling me? What do you need?
jokes.setup: Lucille answers the phone while dealing with a bird
jokes.punchline: Immediately assumes her daughter needs something
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lucille character moment - dismissive about the bird while cynically correct about her daughter's motives.

jokes.text: What is it with this family and working?
jokes.setup: Both Michael and Lucille tell Lindsay to get a job
jokes.punchline: Lindsay treats working like it's an unreasonable family obsession
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character line that perfectly captures privileged entitlement. The idea that employment is a weird family quirk is absurdly perfect.

jokes.text: You run around with everyone else, going on bike rides, making cornholes.
jokes.setup: Lucille complains about activities that exclude Buster
jokes.punchline: Mispronounces 'cornballs' as 'cornholes'
jokes.explanation: Classic malapropism that creates unintended double entendre. Works for Lucille's character - she doesn't quite get the family activities.

jokes.text: Everyone's laughing, riding and cornholing except Buster.
jokes.setup: Lucille continues the cornhole mispronunciation
jokes.punchline: Doubles down with a list that sounds increasingly inappropriate
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the malapropism. The rhythm of the three activities creates a comedic list structure.

jokes.text: This is not going to be a day at the beach. That's cool. Mom packed me a change of clothes.
jokes.setup: Michael warns this will be work, not fun
jokes.punchline: Buster reveals he's prepared for the beach anyway
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing Buster's disconnect from reality and his mother's overprotectiveness. Solid structure.

jokes.text: Yeah. Mom's awesome.
jokes.setup: Gob complains about their mother's controlling manipulation
jokes.punchline: Buster's sincere, oblivious praise
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Buster's complete inability to see his mother's manipulation. Maximum economy.

jokes.text: Maybe we should call her.
jokes.setup: Awkward silence after Buster's mom comment
jokes.punchline: Buster suggests calling the very person they're complaining about
jokes.explanation: Escalates Buster's obliviousness perfectly. Shows his dependency and inability to understand the conversation.

jokes.text: Is this a business decision, or is it personal? 'Cause if it's business, I'll go away happily. But if it's personal, I'll go away. But I won't be happy.
jokes.setup: Gob asks Michael why he can't stay in the office
jokes.punchline: Offers a meaningless choice - he'll leave either way, just with different emotions
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist logic that perfectly captures Gob's character. The false choice structure is brilliant.

jokes.text: It's personal.
jokes.setup: Gob's elaborate question about business vs personal
jokes.punchline: Michael's blunt, immediate answer
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and timing. Michael's deadpan response cuts through Gob's elaborate setup perfectly.

jokes.text: I am so sorry.
jokes.setup: Michael says it's personal
jokes.punchline: Gob's unexpected sincere apology
jokes.explanation: Unexpected character beat showing Gob's rare moment of sincerity, which makes it funny.

jokes.text: Yeah. I just love the theater. That's great. I'm just doing it to kiss Steve Holt.
jokes.setup: George Michael claims to love theater
jokes.punchline: Maeby immediately reveals her shallow motive
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between George Michael's pretense and Maeby's honesty. Sets up the romantic triangle dynamics.

jokes.text: Well, actually, I think I'm gonna quit. Yeah, theater's dead.
jokes.setup: George Michael just claimed to love theater
jokes.punchline: Immediately wants to quit when he learns about Maeby's crush
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing George Michael's immediate deflation. The dramatic pronouncement about theater being dead is funny.

jokes.text: But, no. No. I love the theater... and I gave my word, so I'm back in.
jokes.setup: George Michael just quit the play
jokes.punchline: Changes his mind again when Maeby mentions the stand-in
jokes.explanation: Shows George Michael's indecisiveness and how easily his decisions are swayed by romantic prospects.

jokes.text: Oh, my God.
jokes.setup: Tobias announces he's the new theater director
jokes.punchline: Maeby's horrified reaction to her father taking over
jokes.explanation: Perfect reaction beat capturing the horror of having your dad take over your activity. Maximum economy.

jokes.text: That just makes me wanna puke all over your head, sir.
jokes.setup: Tobias meets with the principal about directing
jokes.punchline: Responds to poor directing with graphic threat
jokes.explanation: Absurdly violent response to bad theater direction. Perfect for Tobias's overwrought dramatic personality.

jokes.text: Sure. Let the little fruit do it.
jokes.setup: Principal asks Jerry if he's okay with Tobias directing
jokes.punchline: Dismissive comment that Tobias doesn't catch as insulting
jokes.explanation: Dark humor showing casual homophobia that Tobias misses completely. Sets up his obliviousness to others' perception of him.

jokes.text: Here I go again.
jokes.setup: Buster is struggling with building the bike
jokes.punchline: Announces another failure with resignation
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Buster's self-aware incompetence and resignation to failure.

jokes.text: Mom told me to stay away from microwaves.
jokes.setup: Michael suggests Buster eat in the snack room
jokes.punchline: Buster's irrational fear of microwaves from mom
jokes.explanation: Perfect example of Lucille's overprotectiveness creating irrational adult fears in Buster. Great character work.

jokes.text: this is gonna be... the most bitchingly fast bike in all of Orange County.
jokes.setup: Buster describes the bike he's building
jokes.punchline: Uses outdated slang 'bitchingly' earnestly
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Buster's outdated slang usage. The earnest delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: not two young men playing grab-ass in the shower.
jokes.setup: Tobias critiques the actors' performance
jokes.punchline: Uses inappropriate shower metaphor for Shakespeare
jokes.explanation: Classic Tobias moment with unintentionally homoerotic imagery while trying to be serious about acting.

jokes.text: You know, I can't go on a million bike rides whenever you want me to.
jokes.setup: Michael wants to go on another bike ride
jokes.punchline: George Michael's dramatic exaggeration of bike ride frequency
jokes.explanation: Good teenage exaggeration showing George Michael's frustration. The 'million' is appropriately dramatic.

jokes.text: Not a girl? Of course.
jokes.setup: George Michael denies it's about a girl
jokes.punchline: Tobias jumps to the wrong conclusion
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup for Tobias misreading the situation. His certainty makes the wrong conclusion funnier.

jokes.text: Methinks a Cupid I shall play.
jokes.setup: Tobias thinks he understands George Michael's situation
jokes.punchline: Speaks in Shakespearean while planning matchmaking
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tobias moment combining his theatrical pretensions with his meddling nature. The Shakespearean language is spot-on.

jokes.text: Fancy that.
jokes.setup: Tobias explains that men played women in Shakespeare's time
jokes.punchline: George Michael's understated response to the awkward situation
jokes.explanation: Good understatement showing George Michael's discomfort. The formal phrasing adds to the awkwardness.

jokes.text: Steve Holt!
jokes.setup: Steve Holt is called upon
jokes.punchline: His automatic self-celebration response
jokes.explanation: Continuation of the running gag. The automatic nature of his response makes it funnier each time.

jokes.text: You're in prison.
jokes.setup: George Sr. says Michael never sees him anymore
jokes.punchline: Michael states the obvious reason
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response to an absurd complaint. The economy is excellent - just stating the obvious fact.

jokes.text: Yeah, and it's tough. We can really only play about two hands.
jokes.setup: Strip poker in prison
jokes.punchline: Limited to two hands because everyone gets naked quickly
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor about prison life. The logic is perfectly twisted - strip poker is limited by how little clothing prisoners have.

jokes.text: Maybe it was the 11 months he spent in the womb.
jokes.setup: George Sr. wonders what went wrong with Buster
jokes.punchline: Suggests abnormally long gestation period
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd explanation for Buster's issues. The specific detail of 11 months makes it funnier than just 'long pregnancy.'

jokes.text: Wow. We're just blowing through nap time, aren't we?
jokes.setup: Heavy conversation about parenting failures
jokes.punchline: George Sr. notes they're past his usual nap time
jokes.explanation: Great deflation of serious moment. Shows George Sr.'s selfishness and breaks tension perfectly.

jokes.text: Are you crazy? I'm a magician. I make my living with my hands.
jokes.setup: Lindsay suggests Gob help with something physical
jokes.punchline: Gob's dramatic overprotection of his hands
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Gob's vanity and self-importance about his magical abilities.

jokes.text: She's not 'that Mexican,' Mom. She's my Mexican.
jokes.setup: Lucille dismissively refers to Marta as 'that Mexican'
jokes.punchline: Gob's attempt to defend her is equally problematic
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment showing Gob's casual racism while thinking he's being protective. The possessive 'my Mexican' is perfectly awful.

jokes.text: If I can't find a horny immigrant by then, I don't deserve to stay here.
jokes.setup: Gob has three days to find a place to stay
jokes.punchline: His crude plan for finding accommodation
jokes.explanation: Dark humor showing Gob's opportunistic approach to relationships. The casual crudeness is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Yeah. And you might be eating... Well, let's hope it doesn't come to that.
jokes.setup: Buster tries to make a comeback about eating dust
jokes.punchline: Can't think of a good retort and gives up mid-sentence
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment showing Buster's inability to complete a thought or comeback. The awkward abandonment is perfect.

jokes.text: I wish I could say the same for Steve Holt. I don't know what the hell her problem is.
jokes.setup: Tobias discusses the actors
jokes.punchline: Refers to Steve Holt as 'her' due to the role reversal
jokes.explanation: Good confusion humor showing Tobias getting lost in his casting choices. Sets up the gender confusion running gag.

jokes.text: It was utterly macabre.
jokes.setup: Gob describes things not working out with his mother
jokes.punchline: Uses overly dramatic vocabulary for a family interaction
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Gob's tendency toward melodrama. 'Macabre' is an amusing word choice for family drama.

jokes.text: I'm Mom, and I wanna shoot down everything you say so I feel good about myself.
jokes.setup: Buster mimics their mother
jokes.punchline: Accurately describes Lucille's psychological pattern
jokes.explanation: Brilliant moment of insight from the usually oblivious Buster. Perfectly captures Lucille's psychology with surprising accuracy.

jokes.text: 'Cause I'm an uptight... Buster! You old horny slut!
jokes.setup: Buster continues his impression
jokes.punchline: Lucille arrives mid-impression with inappropriate greeting
jokes.explanation: Absolutely brilliant timing and escalation. Lucille's inappropriate greeting interrupting Buster's critique is perfectly structured comedy. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: Well, no one's gonna top that.
jokes.setup: Lucille's shocking greeting to Buster
jokes.punchline: Michael acknowledges the comedy peak
jokes.explanation: Good meta moment acknowledging the absurdity. Helps punctuate the comedy peak that came before.

jokes.text: They beat her up, and, uh, took all her clothes.
jokes.setup: Lindsay claims the dress was stolen from Maeby's locker
jokes.punchline: Adds dramatic detail about violence
jokes.explanation: Shows Lindsay's tendency to elaborate lies. The escalation is character-appropriate but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Buster was right. You get off on being withholding, don't you?
jokes.setup: Lucille refuses to help
jokes.punchline: Lindsay repeats Buster's earlier psychological insight
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Buster's earlier insight. Shows the family's newfound ability to see through Lucille's manipulation.

jokes.text: Send her over. I'll take her shopping. Buster was right. You get off on being withholding, don't you?
jokes.setup: Lindsay calls Lucille withholding
jokes.punchline: Lucille immediately proves the point by offering help when attacked
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony - Lucille's withholding nature is so reflexive she reverses when called out on it

jokes.text: Zip me up.
jokes.setup: Parallel to earlier scenes with Lucille and Buster
jokes.punchline: Michael now in the same dependent position
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback showing role reversal. Michael now needs help with dressing, mirroring the earlier dependency dynamics.

jokes.text: I'm flying! Buster?
jokes.setup: Buster built a faster bike but no brakes
jokes.punchline: The joy of flying followed by Michael's concern
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy setup. Buster's joy contrasted with the dangerous situation creates good tension.

jokes.text: No. I'm withholding it. Look at me getting off.
jokes.setup: Lucille offers Buster candy
jokes.punchline: Demonstrates the withholding behavior she was accused of
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback where Lucille embraces the accusation. Her self-awareness and sadistic pleasure is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Michael. I wanna go with Mom.
jokes.setup: Michael tells Buster to make his own decisions
jokes.punchline: Buster immediately chooses dependency
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Buster's inability to embrace independence. The immediate return to mom is perfectly predictable yet funny.

jokes.text: I was trying to fit in.
jokes.setup: Buster criticized his mother earlier
jokes.punchline: Reveals it was just peer pressure, not genuine insight
jokes.explanation: Deflates his earlier moment of insight, showing it was just social conformity. Perfect character consistency.

jokes.text: but a little too close to the sun.
jokes.setup: Buster talks about flying on the bike
jokes.punchline: References Icarus myth inappropriately
jokes.explanation: Good literary reference showing Buster's pretentious education applied to a bike crash. The classical allusion is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Front seat, Mom. I sit in the front seat now. Mmm?
jokes.setup: Buster asserts his independence
jokes.punchline: Tiny rebellion of choosing front seat
jokes.explanation: Perfect Buster moment - his idea of rebellion is choosing the front seat. The 'Mmm?' seeking approval is perfect.

jokes.text: Because they usually don't.
jokes.setup: Lucille's advice about letting children fly back to you
jokes.punchline: Ominous truth about children not returning
jokes.explanation: Dark wisdom from Lucille that undercuts the usual parenting advice. Her cynical honesty is both funny and chilling.

jokes.text: You smell like my mom.
jokes.setup: Maeby finally gets to kiss Steve Holt
jokes.punchline: He ruins the moment with the worst possible comment
jokes.explanation: Perfect romance-killer line. Steve Holt's oblivious comment destroys Maeby's romantic fantasy in the worst possible way.

jokes.text: That's a little cornball. I don't mind. I don't mind either, buddy.
jokes.setup: Father-son reconciliation with emotional honesty
jokes.punchline: Uses 'cornball' as double meaning - corny and literal cornballs
jokes.explanation: Sweet wordplay connecting the emotional sentiment to the episode's physical comedy prop

jokes.text: That's a little cornball.
jokes.setup: George Michael says something sentimental
jokes.punchline: Pun on cornball/Cornballer
jokes.explanation: Nice callback pun connecting the emotional moment to the running Cornballer gag. Good economy and timing.

jokes.text: Mother of God! Oh! Every damn time! Oh! This is a big one. Oh!
jokes.setup: Michael makes cornballs with George Michael
jokes.punchline: Gets burned again by the Cornballer with escalating pain
jokes.explanation: Final Cornballer callback with escalated pain. Good closure for the running gag though not particularly original.

jokes.text: onward and upward! On... Why, Tracy? Why?
jokes.setup: Tobias dismisses a negative review
jokes.punchline: Immediately breaks down dramatically
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tobias moment showing his false bravado immediately crumbling. The dramatic collapse is character-perfect.

jokes.text: Mom! What is it? I can't get down.
jokes.setup: Buster is stuck somewhere high up
jokes.punchline: Back to being completely dependent on mom
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending showing Buster's complete return to dependency. Visual gag reinforces his inability to function independently.
